title: No prose-only theorem policy
purpose: Prevent release-critical theorem claims from existing only as report prose by requiring machine-readable bindings to checker surfaces, proof obligations, gap records, and the active release boundary.

policyRules.description: Every release-critical theorem binding entry must cite at least one binding group with checker files, proof artifacts, and tests.
No theorem-binding entry may discharge public theorem emission or have a public-emission effect under the current boundary.
The proof-obligation and gap ledgers must represent the current theorem spine, including finite-to-unbounded uniformity and unrestricted final soundness as non-activated obligations.
Historical theorem-emission prose remains fenced by the current release ladder, gap ledger, status boundary, and report theorem inventory rather than acting as an active theorem gate.

This seed policy covers the release-critical spine and now records the historical Section 22 theorem-ledger inventory, while explicitly not yet claiming every numbered theorem environment in the historical report has a checker binding.

currentVerdict.reason: The release-critical theorem spine has machine-readable binding and obligation surfaces, and the historical Section 22 theorem-ledger rows are now inventoried. The policy still does not claim exhaustive coverage of every numbered theorem environment or public theorem activation.

nonClaims: This policy does not activate public theorem emission.
This policy does not clear Release.UnrestrictedFinalSoundness or ExternalReview.Acceptance.
This policy does not yet prove that every numbered theorem environment in the historical canonical report is bound.
This policy does not treat report prose as a checker result.
This policy covers the current release-critical theorem spine and preserves the current gap and release-ladder boundaries.
